data.title: Societe Generale Predicts Gold Prices to Reach $6,000 per Ounce by Year-End
data.description: Societe Generale has announced that its previous forecast of gold prices reaching $5,000 per ounce by the end of the year has been met. According to Odaily, the bank now anticipates that gold prices will rise to $6,000 per ounce by year-end. However, it cautions that this estimate might be conservative, suggesting that prices could potentially exceed this level.

data.title: BNB Chain's Prediction Market Volume Surpasses $20 Billion
data.description: BNB Chain's on-chain prediction market has reached a cumulative trading volume exceeding $20 billion. According to Odaily, this milestone was highlighted in a post on the X platform, citing data from Dune.

data.title: SEC and CFTC Reschedule Joint Event to Discuss Cryptocurrency Coordination
data.description: The U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) has officially announced the rescheduling of its joint event with the U.S. Commodity Futures Trading Commission (CFTC). According to Odaily, the event will now take place on January 29, Thursday, from 2 p.m. to 3 p.m. Eastern Time at the CFTC headquarters. The focus of the event will be on the coordination between the two agencies and their efforts to fulfill U.S. President Donald Trump's commitment to establishing the United States as the global hub for cryptocurrency.

data.title: The "Silver Iron-Headed Short Seller" continued to add to his position over the past hour, and is now facing a floating loss of over $6.5 million, with a liquidation price of approximately $125.
data.description: According to MLM monitoring, the "Silver Iron Head Short Seller" has been continuously adding small amounts of positions in the past hour. Currently, its 20x leveraged short position in silver has increased to approximately 361,995 xyz:SILVER contracts, with a floating loss of over $6.5 million and a liquidation price of $125.3542.

data.title: BlackRock drops S-1 filing for iShares Bitcoin Premium Income ETF
data.description:
BlackRock has dropped the official S-1 for its upcoming iShares Bitcoin Premium Income ETF. According to the document, the asset manager filed for an S-1 on January 23, 2026. The filing marks a step toward launching the Bitcoin-focused income ETF under the iShares platform. According to Eric Balchunas, a senior ETF analyst at Bloomberg, the […]
source: https://www.cryptopolitan.com/blackrock-ishares-bitcoin-premium-income-etf/

data.title: Coinbase Derivatives launches copper and platinum futures trading
data.description: Coinbase has announced that its derivatives marketplace, Coinbase Derivatives, has officially launched trading in copper and platinum futures.

data.title: Sky Protocol spent 1.9 million USDS to buy back 29.5 million SKY tokens last week.
data.description: Sky Protocol announced on its X platform that it spent 1.9 million USDS to repurchase 29.5 million SKY tokens last week. Since the program was launched, the cumulative repurchase amount has exceeded 104 million USDS.

data.title: UK Banks Block Nearly 40% of Crypto Exchange Transfers, New Report Finds
data.description:
A new report by the UK Cryptoasset Business Council (UKCBC) and shared by The Block, reveals that major UK retail and commercial banks are currently blocking or delaying close to 40% of all transactions destined for digital asset exchanges, intensifying concerns over a growing “debanking” trend across the country’s crypto sector.
source: https://www.ethnews.com/uk-banks-block-nearly-40-of-crypto-exchange-transfers-new-report-finds/

data.title: Grayscale transferred a total of approximately 46 BTC, 3,959 ETH, and 1,286 SOL to Coinbase.
data.description: According to Arkham's monitoring, about two hours ago, Grayscale transferred a total of approximately 46.676 BTC, 282.541 ETH, and 1286 SOL to a Coinbase Prime address through its CoinDesk Crypto 5 ETF. In addition, Grayscale also transferred 3677 ETH to a Coinbase Prime hot wallet address, with a total value of approximately $15.68 million.

data.title: Goldman Sachs: Investors' risk appetite has risen to a high level, and market sentiment remains optimistic.
data.description: Goldman Sachs research analysts pointed out that despite escalating geopolitical risks, investor risk appetite still increased from December last year to January this year. According to the bank's research, investors are increasing borrowing to support trading, with margin account leverage rising sharply since April last year, currently reaching its highest level since 2021. The research also shows that retail investors are increasingly choosing assets outside the "Big Seven" US stocks, expanding their investment exposure to different regions. Investors are also increasingly shorting the VIX volatility index, with short positions relative to long positions reaching a ten-year high, indicating a widespread market expectation of decreasing volatility. (Jinshi)

data.title: The joint event between the US SEC and CFTC has been rescheduled to January 29.
data.description: The U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) has officially announced that its joint event with the Commodity Futures Trading Commission (CFTC) has been rescheduled to Thursday, January 29, from 2 p.m. to 3 p.m. Eastern Time at CFTC headquarters. The event will discuss coordination between the two agencies and their efforts to realize President Trump's promise to make the United States the world's cryptocurrency capital.

data.title: Ethereum(ETH) Drops Below 2,900 USDT with a 1.19% Decrease in 24 Hours
data.description: On Jan 26, 2026, 15:41 PM(UTC). According to Binance Market Data, Ethereum has dropped below 2,900 USDT and is now trading at 2,897.199951 USDT, with a narrowed 1.19% decrease in 24 hours.

data.title: Senate Agriculture Committee Reschedules Market Structure Bill Hearing Due to Winter Storm
data.description: The Senate Agriculture Committee has postponed its scheduled markup hearing to Thursday morning because of a winter storm. According to NS3.AI, the hearing will concentrate on discussions and voting concerning the market structure bill. This delay could potentially alter the legislative timeline for implementing market structure reforms.

data.title: Consensys Team to Operate MEV Blocker RPC Tool
data.description: CoW DAO has announced that its MEV protection tool, MEV Blocker RPC, will be operated by Consensys' Special Mechanisms Group (SMG). According to PANews, the tool has served over 4.5 million users and returned 6,177 ETH to them. SMG aims to enhance user-centric transparency and expand protection coverage. Meanwhile, CoW DAO will focus on enhancing MEV protection capabilities through CoW Swap and CoW Protocol to expand its decentralized trading platform.

data.title: Will XRP & BTC Prices Chase Up Gold’s New All-Time Highs?
data.description:
Discrepancy between Bitcoin & Gold’s price raises eyebrows: why are major-caps left behind in this surge?
source: https://dailycoin.com/will-xrp-btc-prices-chase-up-golds-new-all-time-highs/?utm_source=cryptopanic&utm_medium=rss

data.title: Benchmark: Delay in the crypto market structure bill could lead to structural limitations on the US crypto market.
data.description: Wall Street brokerage Benchmark stated that the US cryptocurrency market will face structural constraints if Congress fails to pass a market structure bill this year. Analyst Mark Palmer noted in a report that the legislative vacuum will lead to a persistent structural risk premium, limiting the valuation expansion of platforms influenced by the US. This situation will delay the maturation of cryptocurrencies, making investors more inclined towards Bitcoin-centric investments, strong balance sheets, and cash flow-generating infrastructure, rather than regulatory-sensitive areas such as exchanges, decentralized finance (DeFi), and altcoins. DeFi and smart contract platforms are most vulnerable, while Bitcoin, miners, and energy-backed infrastructure are less risky. The bill aims to define market structure by specifying how digital assets are classified as commodities or securities and clarifying the regulatory responsibilities of the Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) and the Commodity Futures Trading Commission (CFTC). Despite the delays, Palmer believes the bill is more likely to pass than fail, and any version of the legislation will reduce regulatory risks and unlock broader institutional participation. (CoinDesk)

data.title: U.S. Crypto Market Faces Structural Constraints Without Congressional Action
data.description: Benchmark, a Wall Street brokerage firm, has indicated that the U.S. cryptocurrency market will face structural limitations if Congress fails to pass a market structure bill this year. According to Odaily, analyst Mark Palmer highlighted in a report that the absence of legislation will lead to a persistent structural risk premium, thereby restricting valuation expansion for platforms influenced by the U.S. This situation is expected to delay the maturation of cryptocurrencies, prompting investors to favor Bitcoin-centric investments, robust balance sheets, and cash flow-generating infrastructure over exchanges, decentralized finance (DeFi), and altcoins, which are sensitive to regulatory changes. DeFi and smart contract platforms are particularly vulnerable, whereas Bitcoin, miners, and energy-supported infrastructure face less risk. The proposed bill aims to define how digital assets are classified as commodities or securities and clarify the regulatory roles of the Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) and the Commodity Futures Trading Commission (CFTC). Despite potential delays, Palmer believes the likelihood of the bill passing is greater than not, and any version of the legislation would reduce regulatory risks and encourage broader institutional participation.

data.title: First Avalanche ETF hits US markets as VanEck launches AVAX fund
data.description:
The first fund to track the Avalanche's AVAX token and include staking rewards debuted Monday with the launch of the VanEck Avalanche ETF.
source: https://www.theblock.co/post/387048/first-avalanche-etf-hits-us-markets-as-vaneck-launches-avax-fund?utm_source=rss&utm_medium=rss

data.title: US Senate Banking Committee Delays CLARITY Act Markup Session
data.description: The U.S. Senate Banking Committee has rescheduled the markup session for the CLARITY Act, a legislative proposal concerning the structure of the cryptocurrency market. According to NS3.AI, the session is now set for January 29 at 3:30 p.m. UTC. Earlier reports suggested that the markup might be postponed until late February or March.

data.title: UK Banks Impose Restrictions on Crypto Transfers, Report Finds
data.description: A recent report highlights that approximately 40% of transfers from major UK banks to cryptocurrency exchanges are either blocked or delayed. According to NS3.AI, eight out of ten leading UK centralized exchanges have faced heightened restrictions over the past year. The UK Crypto-Business Council has criticized banks for leveraging regulatory compliance as a means to curb crypto financial activities, viewing these actions as obstacles to the growth of the industry.
